question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamType.RECORD returned by getStreamType() to craft a scenario where the hash chain verification in shouldParse() throws a HashMismatchException on a record file containing a TOKENUNFREEZE, causing the file to be retried indefinitely while the TOKENFREEZE from a prior file remains committed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getRetry().getMinBackoff()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where the minimum retry backoff of 500ms for a failing TOKENUNFREEZE record file, combined with a high-frequency TOKENFREEZE submission rate, causes the mirror node to always be processing freeze transactions while unfreeze retries are delayed, creating a permanent de-facto freeze state visible to API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getQueueCapacity()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the record stream with 10+ record files (exceeding batch.queueCapacity) all containing TOKENFREEZE transactions, causing queue overflow that drops TOKENUNFREEZE files from the processing queue, resulting in permanent frozen fund state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTransactionTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENFREEZE transaction in a record file that is processed successfully, then submit a TOKENUNFREEZE in a subsequent record file that contains enough additional transactions to push processing time beyond the transactionTimeout (120s), causing the DB transaction to be rolled back and the account to remain permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getMaxFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch.maxFiles (default 1) limit such that when the importer is in catch-up mode and processes files in batches, a TOKENFREEZE file is included in one batch and committed, but the TOKENUNFREEZE file is excluded from the batch due to the maxFiles limit and is processed in a subsequent batch that fails, leaving the account permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getRetry().getMaxAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that retry.maxAttempts defaults to Integer.MAX_VALUE (2,147,483,647) to cause the parser to enter an infinite retry loop on a TOKENUNFREEZE-containing file, consuming all available threads and preventing any new record files from being processed, effectively making all token freezes permanent?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the processingTimeout health check (default 10s) to fire during the retry backoff window of a TOKENUNFREEZE file, triggering a health check failure that causes the importer to be restarted by an orchestration system, resetting the retry counter and causing the unfreeze to never be processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getWindow()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch.window (5 minutes) to ensure that when the importer is more than 5 minutes behind, it enters batch mode and processes a TOKENFREEZE file in a batch, but the TOKENUNFREEZE file is processed outside the batch window in a separate transaction that is rolled back due to a timeout, leaving the freeze permanent?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTransactionTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file where a TOKENFREEZE transaction is near the beginning and a TOKENUNFREEZE is near the end, and by including thousands of complex contract transactions in between, cause the transactionTimeout to expire after the freeze is written to the DB but before the unfreeze is reached, with the partial transaction being rolled back and retried without the unfreeze?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that the setEnabled(boolean) method in ParserProperties allows runtime modification of the enabled flag, and if an attacker can trigger a configuration refresh (e.g., via Spring Cloud Config) that temporarily sets enabled=false during TOKENUNFREEZE processing, the unfreeze file is silently skipped and the account remains frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getRetry().getMultiplier()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a parse failure on a TOKENUNFREEZE record file such that the exponential backoff with multiplier=2 grows to the maxBackoff of 30s, and during this 30s window, the mirror node REST API serves the stale FROZEN status, causing downstream applications to permanently block fund transfers based on the incorrect freeze state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getFlushInterval()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 2-second batch.flushInterval to create a timing attack where a TOKENFREEZE is submitted just before a flush boundary (ensuring it is committed), and a TOKENUNFREEZE is submitted just after the boundary (ensuring it is in the next batch), and by causing the second batch to fail, the freeze state persists permanently?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTransactionTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @DurationMin(seconds = 1) minimum constraint on transactionTimeout to demonstrate that even with the minimum 1-second timeout, a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction in a large record file will consistently time out and roll back, while a TOKENFREEZE in a smaller prior file was already committed, permanently freezing funds?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getMaxItems()] Can an unprivileged external user submit exactly batch.maxItems - 1 (59,999) transactions before a TOKENFREEZE and then submit a TOKENUNFREEZE as the next transaction, causing the freeze to be in one batch and the unfreeze to be in the next batch, and by causing the second batch to fail via a crafted malformed transaction, leave the account permanently frozen?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20ms polling frequency to submit a TOKENFREEZE transaction that is picked up in one poll cycle and committed, then cause a network partition that prevents the TOKENUNFREEZE record file from being downloaded during subsequent poll cycles, resulting in the freeze state persisting for the duration of the partition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getRetry().getMaxAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TOKENUNFREEZE record file that triggers a non-transient exception (e.g., a data integrity violation) on every retry attempt, causing the parser to retry Integer.MAX_VALUE times and never advance past the failing file, permanently blocking all subsequent unfreeze operations for all accounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getQueueCapacity()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch.queueCapacity (default 10) to cause a back-pressure scenario where the queue is full of TOKENFREEZE-containing files, and when a TOKENUNFREEZE file arrives, it is rejected from the queue due to capacity limits, causing the unfreeze to be delayed indefinitely and the freeze state to persist?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTransactionTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @Transactional timeout is specified in seconds (via @DurationUnit(ChronoUnit.SECONDS)) to demonstrate that a transactionTimeout of 1 second (minimum) is insufficient to process a record file containing both a TOKENFREEZE and a TOKENUNFREEZE along with many other transactions, causing the unfreeze to be rolled back while the freeze persists from a prior committed transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getWindow()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch.window (5 minutes) threshold to ensure that when the importer is exactly 5 minutes behind, it switches between batch and non-batch processing modes mid-stream, causing a TOKENFREEZE to be committed in batch mode and a TOKENUNFREEZE to be processed in non-batch mode with a different transaction boundary, and if the non-batch transaction fails, the freeze persists?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getRetry().getMinBackoff()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a scenario where the retry.minBackoff (500ms) for a failing TOKENUNFREEZE file is long enough that the mirror node's REST API serves the stale FROZEN status to a high-frequency trading application, causing it to permanently blacklist the frozen account and refuse all future fund transfers even after the freeze is eventually resolved?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getBatch().getMaxFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the batch.maxFiles (default 1) limit to demonstrate that in batch mode, only one file is processed per batch, meaning a TOKENFREEZE in file N and a TOKENUNFREEZE in file N+1 are always in separate DB transactions, and if the transaction for file N+1 is rolled back, the freeze from file N persists permanently?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: getTransactionTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a TOKENUNFREEZE transaction in a record file that also contains a large number of NFT transfers, causing the DB transaction to exceed the transactionTimeout (120s) and roll back, while a prior TOKENFREEZE in a separate committed transaction remains, permanently freezing the account's fungible token balance?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordParserProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Unintended permanent freezing of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the enabled flag is a simple boolean with no audit trail, such that if an attacker can cause a configuration change that sets enabled=false (e.g., via a misconfigured environment variable), all TOKENUNFREEZE record files are silently skipped, permanently freezing all accounts that were frozen before the flag was changed?
